summary: GWAS Catalog links rs99780-T with Serum metabolite ratios in chronic kidney disease.
detail: GWAS Catalog association with p=2e-16. Effect: OR/Beta=0.356899 [0.27-0.44] unit decrease. Initial sample: 1,143 European ancestry cases.
whyItMatters: This common-variant association is matched locally against the uploaded genotype and should be treated as a tendency signal, not a prediction.
conditions: Serum metabolite ratios in chronic kidney disease
riskSummary: FADS1 risk allele is associated with Serum metabolite ratios in chronic kidney disease (OR/Beta 0.356899 [0.27-0.44] unit decrease) in a large, replicated genome-wide study
notes: Study: Li Y, J Am Soc Nephrol, 2018-03-15.
Generated from bulk GWAS Catalog data; effect size and ancestry transferability require review.

title: Triglyceride levels association near FADS1
summary: GWAS Catalog links rs99780 with Triglyceride levels.
detail: GWAS Catalog association with p=7e-79. Effect: OR/Beta=0.0512 [0.046-0.056] unit increase. Initial sample: 324,694 British ancestry individuals.
whyItMatters: This common-variant association is matched locally against the uploaded genotype and should be treated as a tendency signal, not a prediction.
riskSummary: FADS1 risk allele is associated with Triglyceride levels (OR/Beta 0.0512 [0.046-0.056] unit increase) in a large, replicated genome-wide study
notes: Study: Ripatti P, Circ Genom Precis Med, 2020-03-10.
Generated from bulk GWAS Catalog data; effect size and ancestry transferability require review.

title: White blood cell count association near KLHL2P1
summary: GWAS Catalog links rs9995716 with White blood cell count.
detail: GWAS Catalog association with p=1e-10. Initial sample: approximately 444,000 European ancestry individuals.
whyItMatters: This common-variant association is matched locally against the uploaded genotype and should be treated as a tendency signal, not a prediction.
riskSummary: KLHL2P1 risk allele is associated with White blood cell count in a large, replicated genome-wide study
notes: Study: Kichaev G, Am J Hum Genet, 2018-12-27.
